---
layout: cv
title: Nathan Yearsley's CV
---

# Nathan Yearsley
Business Management Student • General Manager • Team Leader

<div id="webaddress">
<a href="mailto:Nathanyearsley23@gmail.com">Nathanyearsley23@gmail.com</a>
| Idaho Falls, Idaho
| 208-521-0750
</div>

## Profile

Passionate leader with 4 years of team-building and management experience. Skilled in training both managers and crew, organizing operations, and enhancing customer experience. Strong communicator with proficiency in Excel, Word, and other digital tools. Dedicated to developing people, improving processes, and creating a hospitality‑focused environment.

---

## Skills

- **Leadership & Team Development** – Coaching employees, training managers, building high‑performance teams  
- **Customer Service Excellence** – Hospitality focus, customer engagement, experience improvement  
- **Operations Management** – Scheduling, workflow optimization, multi‑tasking under pressure  
- **Training & Onboarding** – Creating training procedures, monitoring compliance, rapid learning  
- **Communication** – Clear communicator with staff, management, and customers  
- **Organization & Documentation** – File management, tracking expenses, maintaining training records  
- **Technical Skills** – Proficient in Excel, Word, and online business tools  
- **Problem‑Solving** – Adapting quickly, handling high‑stress situations, resolving operational issues  

---

## Experience

### General Manager  
**Classic Foods Inc. – Wendy’s, Idaho Falls**  
**April 2021 – Present**

- Lead a diverse team to deliver fast, high‑quality customer service while developing employees of all ages.  
- Coordinate scheduling through effective negotiation and planning to maintain full store coverage.  
- Manage multiple projects and deadlines with flexibility and efficiency.  
- Oversee training across the store to ensure proper procedures and standards are consistently met.  
- Adapt quickly to operational changes and unexpected challenges.  
- Thrive in high‑stress environments while maintaining professionalism and focus.  
- Use Excel to track business performance and profitability.  
- Learn and train new products, systems, and procedures rapidly.  
- Conduct weekly Food Safety Assessments to monitor store progress.  
- Design and implement strategic training procedures for effective onboarding and development.  
- Communicate regularly with upper management regarding goals, progress, and operational updates.  
- Maintain organized employee, training, and expense files for the entire restaurant.  
- Engage with customers to gather feedback and improve overall experience.  
- Build a team culture centered on hospitality and customer satisfaction.

---

## Education

### High School Diploma  
**Idaho Falls High School**  
Class of 2019

### Bachelor of Business Management (Minor in Nutrition)  
**Brigham Young University – Idaho**  
September 2021 – Present  
Anticipated Graduation: Spring 2027

---

## References

**Zachary Peless**  
Phone: 208‑716‑8167  

**Kegan Genther**  
Phone: 208‑821‑4639
